inline_comments.message:
(this is actually my last comment on this class, after I went through all the details below)
I have been think about the correct names for these subscriptions and how best to apply it. Of course the official name is quite a mouthful but maybe if we name the class correctly and then in the (Context of the class) method names and variable names can be shorter?

CmDataJobSubscriptionPersistenceService (is this the correct full name?)

method example:

CmDataJobSubscriptionPersistenceService.isOngoingSubscription(subscriptionId)

What do you think, we can discuss maybe with someone like Priyank too and then agree and apply consistently?
I think you should prefix or postfix with TEMPLATE when these strings contain variables you need to fill in later...
I think you can just call it 'cpsPathQuery' or even 'Query' in the context of thes method and the clearly named constant you are already using
don't think this is needed, try with a unit test
does this not get check earlier already?!

just wondering what happens if thee user tries to remove a non existing id, will it get this far, silently ignored (is behavior defined as part of error handling requirements)? Could be just fine but important it gets tested..
like before you can shorten this

I always think repeating a parameter (name) in the method name is a bit silly (even if inconsistent names are used like here)
But I understand you want to give some context, but is it really needed?
